source.definition: A unit of currency used in several countries, especially in the United States.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "dollar" originates from the term "thaler," which was a silver coin used in Europe. The name "thaler" itself comes from the name of a valley where these coins were first minted in the 16th century.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „dollar” provine de la termenul „thaler”, care era o monedă de argint folosită în Europa. Numele „thaler” vine de la numele unei văi unde aceste monede au fost bătute pentru prima dată în secolul al XVI-lea.

target.definition: Unitate monetară folosită în mai multe țări, în special în Statele Unite ale Americii.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word "dolar" is borrowed from the English "dollar", which originally derives from the term "thaler" (originally "Joachimsthaler").
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „dolar” este împrumutat din engleză „dollar”, care provine la origine din germana „Taler” (din „Joachimsthaler”).

examples.sourceText: The dollar is the official currency of the USA.
examples.targetText: Dolarul este moneda oficială a Statelor Unite ale Americii.

examples.sourceText: The exchange rate of the dollar fluctuates daily.
examples.targetText: Cursul de schimb al dolarului variază zilnic.

examples.sourceText: The dollar is an important reserve currency worldwide.
examples.targetText: Dolarul este o monedă de rezervă importantă la nivel mondial.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word 'dolar' sounds almost the same as the English 'dollar'—this helps you quickly connect the meaning.
The word 'dollar' is borrowed into Romanian from English, which itself comes from an old European coin name—this shows how currency names travel across languages.
Remember the valley where the original silver coins were minted to recall the origin of the word 'dollar'.
Think of counting 'dollars' and associate it with the English word 'dale' (a valley) to anchor the word's origin in your memory.
